agency: Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/dca (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and the NYC Business licensing portal at nyc-business.nyc.gov (Java/Spring web app: `SESSION` cookie, Akamai, Dynatrace). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)' via the Socrata Discovery API (business-license query) and pulled all 37 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' info site (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) + a separate Java/Spring NYC Business licensing portal (nyc-business.nyc.gov) + CityPay (CityBase) payments; 37 Socrata datasets
meta.netnew: File a consumer complaint (ConsumerComplaint) and apply for a business license (LicenseApplication) — the two citizen writes with no API
meta.tagline: DCWP already publishes its entire regulated-business lifecycle openly; the work is binding it into one owned contract and adding the two citizen writes.
meta.headline: The most-open domain yet — 37 datasets cover apply-to-revoke — but no owned API binds them and no API lets a person file a complaint or apply for a license.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a modern Java NYC Business portal + CityPay — maintained but not API-first.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content API; the licensing portal is a session-gated Java app with no documented public API.
scorecard_notes.openData: Exceptional — 37 datasets publish the full licensing/enforcement lifecycle, incl. Issued Licenses (147k views).
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core entities richly modeled and joinable on Business Unique ID / License Number across the lifecycle.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — apply for a license and file a complaint both go through a portal or 311.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; no MCP, no OpenAPI, no schemas published by the agency.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Owns and actively maintains its open datasets, but the transaction layer runs on a shared NYC Business portal it does not solely control.

headline_findings: DCWP is the most-open domain in this project: 37 NYC Open Data datasets publish the entire regulated-business lifecycle — license applications, issued licenses (147k views), inspections, charges, consumer complaints, revocations, and Office of Labor Policy & Standards worker-protection matters.

The whole lifecycle is joinable on `Business Unique ID` and `License Number`, but no single owned API binds those 37 datasets into one coherent resource model — a consumer or agent must learn 37 Socrata IDs.

The two things a person actually DOES have no API: applying for a business license lives only in the Java NYC Business portal (nyc-business.nyc.gov), and filing a consumer complaint lives only in 311 or a DCWP web form.
Worker-protection data (OLPS inquiries and enforcement matters) is published only in aggregate by topic/industry/geography — never an individual worker.

Net-new write surface: file a consumer complaint (ConsumerComplaint) and apply for a license (LicenseApplication) — both have an Open Data READ twin but no write API. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 37 DCWP datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DCWP API — the full licensing/enforcement lifecycle as reference data.
Apply for and manage DCWP licenses. `SESSION` cookie, Akamai edge, Dynatrace RUM; no JSON/OpenAPI surface.
Pay DCWP fines/fees; CityBase-hosted checkout, returns 403 to non-browser clients.
The intake path for consumer complaints against businesses; no public write API to DCWP.
Content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.
`server-timing: ak_p` across the info site and NYC Business portal.

fruit.name: Issued Licenses
fruit.notes: Core Licensee record. Keyed on License Number + Business Unique ID; full address + geography spine, license type/status, issuance & expiration.

fruit.name: License Applications
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data (SODA) for READ; portal-only for WRITE
fruit.notes: Intake record before an issued license. READ is open; SUBMITTING an application lives only in the Java NYC Business portal — a net-new write.

fruit.name: DCWP Inspections
fruit.notes: Inspections of licensed/regulated businesses; keyed on Inspection Number, joins to charges via NOH Number.

fruit.name: DCWP Charges (violations)
fruit.notes: Enforcement record — cited violations, hearing outcomes (guilty/dismissed/cured). Joins to inspections and complaints.

fruit.name: Consumer Complaints
fruit.machine_readable: Open Data (SODA) for READ; 311/web-form only for WRITE
fruit.notes: The citizen-facing transaction. Published records are open; FILING a complaint has no API — 311 or a web form only. The primary net-new WRITE surface.

fruit.name: License Revocations, Suspensions & Reinstatements
opendata_match: License Revocations, Suspensions, Surrenders, and Reinstatements rpeq-j89e (7c)
fruit.notes: License lifecycle events; keyed on DCA License Number + Event Type/Date.

fruit.name: Worker Protection (OLPS)
fruit.notes: The 'Worker' half of DCWP — paid safe/sick leave, fair workweek. Published as AGGREGATE rollups by topic/industry/geography; never an individual worker.

fruit.name: DCWP Licensed Vehicles
fruit.notes: Vehicles/assets tied to a license (e.g. tow trucks, pedicabs); keyed on Business Asset ID + License Number.

fruit.name: Apply for a business license
fruit.notes: Submitting a license application lives only in the Java NYC Business portal; no write API. A net-new write surface.

fruit.name: File a consumer complaint
fruit.notes: The single most common citizen transaction — file a complaint against a business. Today only via 311 or a DCWP web form; no API. The primary net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Pay a fine / fee
fruit.notes: Fine/fee payment lives only in CityPay (CityBase); no public API. Payments Received are published after the fact.
